summary:
At birth, the condylar cartilage is well-formed as it has been developing since early fetal life; thus, any description matching typical adult-like structures would be most appropriate for this age stage based purely off developmental timelines provided here.
The condyles are typically represented by mature-looking hyaline cartilages at birth due to their ongoing growth trajectory from an initial appearance near six weeks post-conception through full-term gestation. To pinpoint exact characteristics among listed options requires more detailed anatomical distinctions not explicitly covered but generally implies they resemble advanced forms found closer to term compared starting materials earlier specified within these references.
